one_time_description: Gain on disposal of capital assets
economic_notes: ACDLP operates two convenience stores, a cafe, a tire shop and a canteen. Cree Nation has a claim against Government of Canada for damages, funded by a loan with insurance coverage. TLE land purchases ongoing. Water treatment plant upgrade and school capital projects underway.
notes: Consolidated entity includes Ahtahkakoop Cree Nation, CMHC Housing, TLE Board Operating Account and TLE Revenue Account. Ahtahkakoop Cree Developments LP (ACDLP) accounted for by modified equity method (GBE). Ahtahkakoop Treaty Land Entitlement Trust excluded from consolidation as Chief and Council do not control the Trust. Emphasis of matter paragraph regarding claim against Government of Canada funded by loan with insurance. New PS 3400 revenue standard adopted prospectively April 1, 2023. Asset retirement obligation change in estimate reduced liability by $746,957. RBC Powwow Arbour Term Loan of $2,480,000 added during year. Current portion of long-term debt is $5,540,874 including the Powwow Arbour loan due July 2024. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + CMHC + Other Government Agencies + Battle River Treaty 6 Health Centre + Battlefords Agency Tribal Chiefs + First Nations Trust + Saskatchewan Indian Institute of Technologies.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (80.06%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
